title: SSE session identifiers route but never authenticate

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
Two opaque authenticated principals have deterministic, independent active, close-target, expiry-target, cross-principal, and inactive-stream SSE routing entries plus body-limit controls.

fault_script.action: Provision distinct synthetic close-target, expiry-target, and inactive-stream entries for opaque-principal-a plus a cross-principal entry for opaque-principal-b; do not create the unknown identifier.
fault_script.expected_effect: Each case has its own routing identifier and lifecycle; no unknown, closed, expired, or cross-principal result can stand in for another.

fault_script.action: Close only routing identifier 22222222-2222-4222-8222-222222222222 and remove its principal binding before the next POST.
fault_script.expected_effect: The dedicated closed identifier returns the same 404 Not Found response as the independently exercised unknown identifier while other entries remain active.

fault_script.action: Advance the fake monotonic clock past the idle deadline only for routing identifier 33333333-3333-4333-8333-333333333333 and run its normal expiry cleanup.
fault_script.expected_effect: The dedicated expired identifier returns the same 404 Not Found response as unknown and closed identifiers while other entries remain active.

fault_script.action: Arm a one-shot close for routing identifier 55555555-5555-4555-8555-555555555555 after the next POST completes routing lookup and before the installed SDK handles the parsed message.
fault_script.expected_effect: The installed SDK writes exactly one 500 SSE connection not established response and the adapter makes no second write.

fault_script.action: Clear the consumed one-shot inactive-stream close injection before duplicate close and server shutdown.
fault_script.expected_effect: No later routing or cleanup operation inherits the inactive-stream fault; ordinary idempotent close and shutdown execute from the recovered fixture state.

steps.expect: Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Bad Request and Connection: close before routing lookup, body read, or dispatch.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Bad Request and Connection: close before routing lookup, body read, or dispatch.
Adapter-owned HTTP 404 with body Not Found and Connection: close; body read and dispatch counts are zero.
Adapter-owned HTTP 404 with body Not Found and Connection: close, indistinguishable from the independent unknown case; body read and dispatch counts are zero.
Adapter-owned HTTP 404 with body Not Found and Connection: close, indistinguishable from the independent unknown and closed cases; body read and dispatch counts are zero.
Adapter-owned HTTP 404 with body Not Found and Connection: close, indistinguishable from the independent unknown, closed, and expired cases; body read and dispatch counts are zero.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Invalid Content-Type and Connection: close before body read or dispatch.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Request body too large and Connection: close; body read count 0 and dispatch count 0.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Request body too large and Connection: close when the first byte beyond 1 MiB arrives; dispatch count 0.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Invalid UTF-8 after the bounded read; dispatch count 0 and no Connection: close header.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Invalid JSON after the bounded read; dispatch count 0 and no Connection: close header.
Adapter-owned HTTP 400 with body Invalid JSON-RPC message after the bounded read; dispatch count 0 and no Connection: close header.
SDK-owned HTTP 202 with body Accepted after exactly one parsed-message dispatch to the active bound transport.
Exactly one SDK-owned HTTP 500 with body SSE connection not established and no adapter second write after headers.
Duplicate close and server shutdown remove each remaining routing entry and principal binding exactly once, make no second response write, and leave the explicit device session independent.

pass.assertion: open-routing-entry returns HTTP 200 and the exact synthetic endpoint routing identifier.
post-missing-routing-id returns exactly 400 Bad Request with Connection: close before lookup or body dispatch.
post-malformed-routing-id independently returns exactly 400 Bad Request with Connection: close before lookup or body dispatch.
post-unknown-routing-id independently returns exactly 404 Not Found with Connection: close.
post-closed-routing-id independently returns the same 404 Not Found body and close semantics as unknown.
post-expired-routing-id independently returns the same 404 Not Found body and close semantics as unknown and closed.
post-cross-principal-routing-id independently returns the same 404 Not Found body and close semantics without transferring principal binding.
post-invalid-media-type returns exactly 400 Invalid Content-Type with Connection: close and zero body dispatch.
post-declared-body-overflow returns exactly 400 Request body too large with Connection: close, zero body reads, and zero dispatches.
post-streamed-body-overflow independently returns exactly 400 Request body too large with Connection: close and zero dispatches.
post-invalid-utf8-body returns exactly 400 Invalid UTF-8 with zero dispatches and no Connection: close header.
post-invalid-json-body independently returns exactly 400 Invalid JSON with zero dispatches and no Connection: close header.
post-invalid-jsonrpc-message independently returns exactly 400 Invalid JSON-RPC message with zero dispatches and no Connection: close header.
post-accepted-message returns exactly 202 Accepted after one bounded parse and one dispatch.
post-inactive-sdk-stream preserves exactly one SDK-owned 500 SSE connection not established response with no second write.
close-routing-entry removes each entry and principal binding once across duplicate callbacks and server shutdown without closing the device session.
Across all independent cases, a routing identifier grants no authentication, authorization, ownership, principal transfer, or device-session identity.

evidence.source: open-routing-entry: 200 text/event-stream with the exact synthetic endpoint identifier.
post-missing-routing-id: 400 Bad Request, Connection: close, zero lookup/read/dispatch.
post-malformed-routing-id: independent 400 Bad Request, Connection: close, zero lookup/read/dispatch.
post-unknown-routing-id: 404 Not Found, Connection: close, zero body read/dispatch.
post-closed-routing-id: independent 404 Not Found with the same close semantics.
post-expired-routing-id: independent 404 Not Found with the same close semantics.
post-cross-principal-routing-id: independent 404 Not Found with the same close semantics and no principal transfer.
post-invalid-media-type: 400 Invalid Content-Type, Connection: close, zero body read/dispatch.
post-declared-body-overflow: 400 Request body too large, Connection: close, zero body reads/dispatches.
post-streamed-body-overflow: independent 400 Request body too large, Connection: close, zero dispatches.
post-invalid-utf8-body: 400 Invalid UTF-8, zero dispatches, no Connection: close header.
post-invalid-json-body: independent 400 Invalid JSON, zero dispatches, no Connection: close header.
post-invalid-jsonrpc-message: independent 400 Invalid JSON-RPC message, zero dispatches, no Connection: close header.
post-accepted-message: 202 Accepted after one bounded parse and one dispatch.
post-inactive-sdk-stream: exactly one SDK-owned 500 SSE connection not established response and zero adapter second writes.
close-routing-entry: one removal per entry and principal binding across two callbacks and server shutdown.
All independent routing cases retain the authenticated principal boundary and device-session independence.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
